text:
On a bright summer morning in a quiet seaside town, a peculiar thing happened. The sand was as golden as the sun itself, and seagulls floated lazily overhead, singing in unison the sweet melodies of the sea.

Margaret was on her way to meet her friend Paul. She had heard tales of Paul's incredible talent for spontaneous poetry, and her curiosity about his work was only matched by her anticipation. Paul lived on the outskirts of town, in a cottage that seemed made of clouds and fairy dust.

Paul was supposed to be standing by the old lighthouse, but when Margaret arrived, he was not there. Instead, she spotted him lounging on a recliner, sipping a glass of ice-cold milk.

"Paul!" Margaret squealed, her eyes wide in excitement.

Paul stood up, scratched his unkempt beard, and grinned. "Margaret! My favorite sight. What brings you here?"

"I've heard you've written some poems that can move the heart
